description: Add Chrome API browser tools: window management, downloads, history search, bookmark management, recently closed tabs, and clear site data. These use chrome.* extension APIs and require adding new permissions to manifest.json. Each tool includes E2E tests.

userStories.title: Add window management tools
userStories.description: Add tools for creating, resizing, listing, and closing browser windows using the chrome.windows API (no new permissions needed — already covered by existing tabs permission).
acceptanceCriteria: browser_list_windows returns all open windows with id, state, bounds (left/top/width/height), tabs count, focused status
browser_create_window accepts url, width, height, left, top, state (normal/minimized/maximized/fullscreen), incognito flag
browser_update_window accepts windowId, state, bounds — resizes/repositions/minimizes/maximizes
browser_close_window accepts windowId — closes the window
All tools defined with group 'Windows', icon 'app-window'
All shared file registrations complete for all 4 tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/list-windows.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/create-window.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/update-window.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/close-window.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/window-commands.ts (NEW — all 4 handlers)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/window-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add 4 exports)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add 4 handler mappings)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add 4 to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add 4 mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add 4 imports + array entries)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
chrome.windows API is straightforward:
- `chrome.windows.getAll({ populate: false })` → list windows
- `chrome.windows.create({ url, width, height, left, top, state, incognito })` → create
- `chrome.windows.update(windowId, { state, left, top, width, height, focused })` → update
- `chrome.windows.remove(windowId)` → close

For list, include `tabCount` by getting `chrome.tabs.query({ windowId })` count for each window.

Server methods: `browser.listWindows`, `browser.createWindow`, `browser.updateWindow`, `browser.closeWindow`

Constraints:
- No new permissions needed — `tabs` permission already covers window access
- Cannot create incognito windows without the `incognito` manifest permission (return a clear error if attempted)
- Window state 'fullscreen' may not work on all platforms

userStories.title: Add download management tools and downloads permission
userStories.description: Add manifest 'downloads' permission and tools for initiating, listing, and monitoring downloads via chrome.downloads API.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json includes 'downloads' permission
browser_download_file accepts url, optional filename, optional saveAs prompt — initiates a download, returns downloadId
browser_list_downloads accepts optional query (filename, url, state), limit — returns recent downloads with id, filename, url, state, bytesReceived, totalBytes, startTime
browser_get_download_status accepts downloadId — returns current state, progress, filepath
All tools defined with group 'Downloads', icon 'download'
All shared file registrations complete for all 3 tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/manifest.json (add 'downloads' permission)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/download-file.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/list-downloads.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-download-status.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/download-commands.ts (NEW — all 3 handlers)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/download-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add 3 exports)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add 3 handler mappings)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add 3 to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add 3 mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add 3 imports + array entries)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
chrome.downloads API:
- `chrome.downloads.download({ url, filename, saveAs })` → returns downloadId
- `chrome.downloads.search({ query, limit, orderBy })` → returns DownloadItem[]
- `chrome.downloads.search({ id })` → returns single item status

DownloadItem has: id, url, filename, state ('in_progress', 'interrupted', 'complete'), bytesReceived, totalBytes, startTime, endTime, error.

For browser_download_file, set `saveAs: false` by default (no user prompt). Return the downloadId so the agent can check progress later.

For browser_list_downloads, default to last 20 downloads sorted by startTime descending.

Server methods: `browser.downloadFile`, `browser.listDownloads`, `browser.getDownloadStatus`

userStories.title: Add history search tools and history permission
userStories.description: Add manifest 'history' permission and tools for searching browser history via chrome.history API.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json includes 'history' permission
browser_search_history accepts text query, optional maxResults (default 20), optional startTime/endTime as ISO strings — returns matching history entries with url, title, visitCount, lastVisitTime
browser_get_visits accepts url — returns detailed visit info with transition types and timestamps
All tools defined with group 'History', icon 'history'
All shared file registrations complete for both tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/manifest.json (add 'history' permission)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/search-history.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-visits.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/history-commands.ts (NEW — both handlers)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/history-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add 2 exports)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add 2 handler mappings)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add 2 to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add 2 mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add 2 imports + array entries)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
chrome.history API:
- `chrome.history.search({ text, maxResults, startTime, endTime })` → HistoryItem[]
- `chrome.history.getVisits({ url })` → VisitItem[] (each has visitId, visitTime, transition)

HistoryItem: { id, url, title, lastVisitTime, visitCount, typedCount }
VisitItem: { id, visitId, visitTime, referringVisitId, transition }

Convert timestamps to ISO strings for the response. The `text` parameter matches URL and title.

Server methods: `browser.searchHistory`, `browser.getVisits`

Constraints:
- Timestamps from chrome.history are ms-since-epoch — convert to ISO strings
- The startTime/endTime parameters in Zod should accept ISO date strings and convert to ms-since-epoch before calling the API

userStories.title: Add bookmark management tools and bookmarks permission
userStories.description: Add manifest 'bookmarks' permission and tools for searching, creating, and listing bookmarks via chrome.bookmarks API.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json includes 'bookmarks' permission
browser_search_bookmarks accepts query string — returns matching bookmarks with id, title, url, parentId, dateAdded
browser_create_bookmark accepts title, url, optional parentId — creates a bookmark, returns the created node
browser_list_bookmark_tree accepts optional parentId — returns bookmark tree (or subtree)
All tools defined with group 'Bookmarks', icon 'bookmark'
All shared file registrations complete for all 3 tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/manifest.json (add 'bookmarks' permission)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/search-bookmarks.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/create-bookmark.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/list-bookmark-tree.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/bookmark-commands.ts (NEW — all 3 handlers)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/bookmark-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add 3 exports)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add 3 handler mappings)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add 3 to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add 3 mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add 3 imports + array entries)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
chrome.bookmarks API:
- `chrome.bookmarks.search(query)` → BookmarkTreeNode[]
- `chrome.bookmarks.create({ parentId, title, url })` → BookmarkTreeNode
- `chrome.bookmarks.getTree()` or `chrome.bookmarks.getSubTree(id)` → BookmarkTreeNode[]

BookmarkTreeNode: { id, parentId, index, url, title, dateAdded, dateGroupModified, children }

For list_bookmark_tree, if no parentId given, return the full tree. Flatten or limit depth to avoid huge responses — max depth 3 by default.

Server methods: `browser.searchBookmarks`, `browser.createBookmark`, `browser.listBookmarkTree`

userStories.title: Add recently closed tabs and clear site data tools
userStories.description: Add tools for restoring recently closed tabs (chrome.sessions) and clearing site data (chrome.browsingData). Add both new permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json includes 'sessions' and 'browsingData' permissions
browser_get_recently_closed returns last 25 recently closed tabs/windows with title, url, closedAt
browser_restore_session accepts sessionId — restores the closed tab/window
browser_clear_site_data accepts origin, and data types to clear (cookies, localStorage, cache, indexedDB, serviceWorkers) — clears selected data
All tools defined with appropriate groups and icons
All shared file registrations complete for all 3 tools
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/manifest.json (add 'sessions' and 'browsingData' permissions)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-recently-closed.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/restore-session.ts (NEW)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/clear-site-data.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/session-commands.ts (NEW — recently closed + restore)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/browsing-data-commands.ts (NEW — clear site data)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/session-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/browsing-data-commands.test.ts (NEW)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add 3 exports)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (add 3 handler mappings)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts (add 3 to DISPATCH_METHODS)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add 3 mocks)
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (add 3 imports + array entries)
- platform/shared/src/generated/browser-tools-catalog.ts (regenerated)

Context:
chrome.sessions API:
- `chrome.sessions.getRecentlyClosed({ maxResults: 25 })` → Session[] (each has tab or window)
- `chrome.sessions.restore(sessionId)` → restored Session

chrome.browsingData API:
- `chrome.browsingData.remove(options, dataToRemove)` where options = `{ origins: ['https://example.com'] }` and dataToRemove = `{ cookies: true, localStorage: true, cache: true, indexedDB: true, serviceWorkers: true }`

For recently closed: convert session timestamps to ISO. Each session has either `.tab` (with url, title) or `.window` (with tabs array).

For clear site data Zod schema:
```typescript
z.object({
  origin: z.string().url().describe('Origin to clear data for (e.g., https://example.com)'),
  cookies: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear cookies (default: true)'),
  localStorage: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear localStorage (default: true)'),
  cache: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear cache (default: false)'),
  indexedDB: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear IndexedDB (default: false)'),
  serviceWorkers: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear service workers (default: false)'),
})
```

Server methods: `browser.getRecentlyClosed`, `browser.restoreSession`, `browser.clearSiteData`
Groups: 'Sessions' (icon 'undo-2') for recently closed/restore, 'Data' (icon 'eraser') for clear site data

userStories.title: E2E tests for Chrome API tools
userStories.description: Add comprehensive E2E tests for all Chrome API browser tools: windows, downloads, history, bookmarks, recently closed, and clear site data.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/browser-chrome-apis.e2e.ts exists with tests for all tool groups
All new tools appear in tools/list
Window tools: create window → list windows shows it → close window
Download tools: download a file from test server → list downloads shows it → check status
History tools: navigate to test server → search history finds the URL
Bookmark tools: create bookmark → search bookmarks finds it
Recently closed: close a tab → get recently closed shows it
Clear site data: returns success for a valid origin
Error cases: invalid parameters return errors
